meta.title: GitHub Profile README — "Wow Factor" Overhaul
meta.author: Ahmed Taha (SufficientDaikon)
meta.objective: Transform an existing GitHub profile README into a state-of-the-art, high-impact developer portfolio that makes visitors go "wow" within 3 seconds of landing.

existing_assets.asset: Custom SVG — 167,471 lines of code bar chart with gradient bars per language
GitHub Action generating snake animation daily to output branch

repo.name: aether
repo.desc: Autonomous Agent Orchestration Framework — multi-agent LLM coordination with 28 subsystems, built on Bun

repo.name: omniskill
repo.desc: Universal AI Agent & Skills Framework — 48 skills, 8 bundles, 7 agents, 5 pipelines. One repo, one format, every platform.

repo.name: sdd-vscode-agents
repo.desc: 13 Copilot Chat agents for Spec-Driven Development & UI/UX Design Lifecycle

repo.name: harvesthub
repo.desc: Adaptive product scraping platform — confidence scoring, premium exports, cron scheduling

repo.name: hugbrowse
repo.desc: Local AI Platform — Browse, download, run Hugging Face models. Privacy-first. Tauri v2 + React + Rust

repo.name: Axon
repo.desc: ML/AI-first systems programming language — custom compiler with lexer, parser, type checker & borrow checker

principles.principle: A visitor must understand who you are, what you build, and be impressed — all within 3 seconds of landing.
Prefer visual proof (numbers, charts, cards) over prose. Every claim should have a visible metric backing it.
Every section must justify its screen real estate. If it doesn't make the visitor more impressed, it's noise — collapse or remove it.
Order sections by impressiveness. Your strongest metrics and best projects come first. Weak stats are hidden or removed.
Social links, alt text, consistent theming, no broken images. This is a portfolio, not a playground.
A profile without contact links looks like a throwaway. LinkedIn + Email minimum.

step.title: Hero Header — Wave + Typing SVG
step.description: Keep the existing capsule-render waving header and typing SVG. They work well. No changes needed to these elements.

step.title: Hero Stat Strip — Shields.io Badge Row
step.description: Add a row of custom shields.io badges immediately after the typing SVG, before the profile views counter. These are the first hard numbers anyone sees. Use flat-square style with #A177FE background for consistency.
step.placement:
After typing SVG tag, before
and profile views counter
step.format: All badges in a single centered div, separated by single spaces. Use shields.io static badge format: https://img.shields.io/badge/{label}-{value}-{color}?style=for-the-badge&logo={logo}&logoColor=white Encode spaces in label as %20, plus signs as %2B.

step.title: Activate Social Badges — LinkedIn + Email
step.description: Replace the commented-out social badge block with active LinkedIn and Email badges. Remove the X/Twitter and Portfolio placeholders (no URLs for those). Keep profile views counter on a separate line above the social links.
step.placement: After profile views counter, still inside the centered header div
step.remove: Entire commented-out social badge HTML block (lines 14-21 of current file)

step.title: Compress About Me — Kill the YAML Wall
step.description: Replace the 20-line YAML code block with a tight, scannable format: - 1 punchy intro sentence (who you are + what you do) - 4 emoji bullet points (domains, not lists) - 1 philosophy quote (keep — it's a great line) Total: ~8 lines max. Readable in 3 seconds.
step.output_format:
``` ### 🧠 About Me
**AI Agent Architect** from Egypt — I build autonomous systems that build software.
- 🤖 LLM orchestration & multi-agent frameworks (48 skills, 7 agents, 5 pipelines) - 🦀 Systems programming — built a compiler with lexer, parser & borrow checker in Rust - 🖥️ Full-stack apps with Tauri, React, Django, and Bun - 🕷️ Data pipelines & web scraping with confidence scoring
> _"If it can be automated, it should be orchestrated by agents."_ ```

step.title: Add "Currently Working On" Section
step.description: Short section showing active work. 3 items max. Uses emoji + bold project name + one-line context. Goes immediately after About Me. Shows the profile is alive, not abandoned.
step.placement: After About Me, before Featured Projects
step.output_format:
``` ### 🔭 Currently Working On
- 🌀 **[Aether](https://github.com/SufficientDaikon/aether)** — Scaling to 28+ subsystems for autonomous agent orchestration - 🧩 **[OMNISKILL](https://github.com/SufficientDaikon/omniskill)** — Expanding cross-platform AI skills to 48+ across 5 editors - 🧬 **[Axon](https://github.com/SufficientDaikon/Axon)** — Building a complete ML/AI-first language compiler in Rust ```

step.title: Replace Project Table with Visual Repo Pin Cards
step.description: Replace the HTML table of projects with github-readme-stats repo pin cards. These auto-render the repo name, description, language, stars, and forks as styled SVG cards. Use the self-hosted instance (grs-deploy.vercel.app) for reliability. Layout: 2 cards per row, 3 rows = 6 projects.
step.layout: Each card wrapped in an <a> tag linking to the repo. Two cards per <div align="center"> row, each with width="49%". Three rows total. Row 1: aether, omniskill Row 2: sdd-vscode-agents, harvesthub Row 3: hugbrowse, Axon

step.title: Tech Stack — No Changes
step.description: Keep the skillicons.dev icon rows exactly as they are. They're visually clean and render reliably.

step.title: Streak & Activity — No Changes
step.description: Keep the side-by-side streak + productive-time cards. They show impressive commit volume and coding hours.

step.title: Languages & Lines of Code — No Changes
step.description: Keep the donut chart + custom LOC SVG side-by-side. The LOC chart is a unique differentiator.

step.title: Contribution Graph — No Changes
step.description: Keep the full-width activity graph. It shows consistent daily activity and is visually impressive.

step.title: Remove Trophies Section
step.description: Delete the entire Trophies section (### 🏆 Trophies through closing and trailing ---). Reason: Current ranks are C/B which undermine the narrative of being prolific. Can be re-added later when rank improves through stars and PRs.

step.title: Collapse Snake into Details/Summary
step.description: Wrap the snake animation section in a <details> tag so it's collapsible. Use a fun summary line to entice clicks. The snake is cool but doesn't justify forcing a full scroll.
step.output_format:
```html
🐍 Watch the contribution snake eat my graph
```

step.title: Footer — No Changes
step.description: Keep the tagline ("I build AI agents that build software") and the capsule-render footer wave. Clean closer.

final_section_order.section: Hero stat badges (167K+ LOC, 1,843 Commits, 48 Skills, 7 Agents, 6 Projects) [NEW]
Featured Projects [UPGRADED — visual repo pin cards]

quality_gates.gate: All images must have descriptive alt text for accessibility and SEO.
All external service URLs must be verified reachable (no 404/503) before committing.
Color theme must be consistent — #A177FE accent, #0D1117 bg, tokyonight variant across all cards.
No commented-out code or placeholder URLs in the final output.
The full README must render correctly on GitHub (test by viewing the deployed profile page).
Total vertical scroll on desktop should be reduced compared to the current version.
The "3-second test" — a new visitor should see: name, title, impressive numbers, and social links without scrolling.

anti_patterns.avoid: Showing stats where the numbers are zero or low (stars: 0, PRs: 0, issues: 0)
Multiple sections showing the same data in different formats (redundant language charts)
Commented-out placeholder sections that signal incompleteness
Trophy/rank sections where ranks are below A tier
Walls of monospace text (YAML/JSON "about me" blocks longer than 8 lines)
Using shared public Vercel instances that frequently 503 (use self-hosted or verified mirrors)
